differential_diagnoses: Metastatic malignancy (occult solid tumor with bone marrow infiltration)
next_steps: Perform bone marrow biopsy with aspiration for cytogenetics, flow cytometry, and immunohistochemistry
Assess for HLH using HScore and serum soluble IL-2 receptor testing
Urgent imaging of the spine (MRI) to evaluate for bone metastases and potential cord compression
Computed tomography of the chest to investigate pulmonary nodules and potential primary malignancy
Serum triglycerides and fibrinogen monitoring to further assess for HLH criteria
Discontinue empirical therapies that are not indicated until a definitive diagnosis is reached

differential_diagnoses: Metastatic adenocarcinoma with bone marrow infiltration (myelophthisis)
Occult primary malignancy (likely lung or pancreatic origin)
next_steps: Bone marrow biopsy and aspiration with flow cytometry, cytogenetics, and iron stains
Systemic HLH workup including soluble CD25 (sIL-2R) and triglyceride levels
Urgent MRI of the total spine to assess for metastatic disease and cord compression
Contrast-enhanced CT of the chest to further characterize lung nodules
Serum tumor markers including CEA, CA 19-9, and CA-125
Discontinuation of empirical immunosuppression until biopsy results are available
Serial monitoring of coagulation profile and fibrinogen to evaluate for consumptive coagulopathy
